title: Polestar Secures Equity Investments from Multiple Partners
text: Polestar announced total of USD 300 million in equity investments from purchasers including Crédit Agricole CIB, Vida Finance S.A., Innovator Limited, and Proximastar Holdings Company Limited. Additionally, Volvo Cars agreed to convert approximately USD 274 million of its outstanding shareholder loan into Polestar's equity. These capital infusions strengthen Polestar's balance sheet and provide runway for operations and new model launches. The multiple equity partners demonstrate confidence in Polestar's long-term strategic direction despite near-term profitability challenges. Geely remains the largest shareholder with 38.21% ownership. The equity raises reduce immediate refinancing risk and dilute existing shareholders less severely than debt conversions alone. The funding supports retail network expansion, with plans to grow from 230 to approximately 250 sales points by year-end 2026. Management views the capital raises as validation of the company's strategy to become a leading premium EV manufacturer. The company estimates needing to reach 100,000+ annual units to achieve profitability. Current profitability timeline remains uncertain, with the company having pushed breakeven targets from 2025 to 2027 and then suspending formal guidance.
